items.summary: bool_to_bin: the 0/1 indicator (for scope_count).
zero64: the Binary{64} zero (seed/trial placeholder in golden/differential FailRecords — Rust sets seed: 0, trial: 0 there).
vec_len: the number of elements as Binary{8}; add_u refuses at 256 on all paths — never a silent wrap (G2; the std.collections `len` contract, Empirical).
rng_new: construct the initial state from a fixed seed; 0 is promoted (never a silent degenerate state). Exact.

rng_next: advance the state and return it — Xorshift64 (x ^= x<<13; x ^= x>>7; x ^= x<<17), bit-exact vs Rust's Rng::next_u64 (whose output IS its new state). Exact: deterministic; the same state always yields the same output (C4/RT3).

rng_next_u32: the high 32 bits of the next draw, as Binary{32} — mirrors Rust's `(next_u64() >> 32) as u32`. The narrow is in-range by construction (the high 32 bits are zero after the shift), so the never-silent width_cast prim narrows Exactly (its second operand is a width witness whose bits are ignored). The caller threads the advanced state via rng_next.
budget_trials: the number of trials this budget permits. Exact.
budget_default: the default budget (100 trials — Budget::DEFAULT). Exact.
budget_min: the minimum budget (1 trial — Budget::MIN). Exact.

items.signature: fn find_failing[T](candidates: Vec[T], prop: T => Bool) => Option[T]
fn shrink_loop[T](best: T, candidates: Vec[T], shrink: T => Vec[T], prop: T => Bool, steps: Binary{16}) => T
items.summary: shrink_loop: descend through shrink candidates keeping the smallest still-failing value; the step count is bounded (Rust's literal 1000, at Binary{16}) so shrinking itself is bounded (C6).

items.signature: fn shrink_to_minimal[T](initial: T, shrink: T => Vec[T], prop: T => Bool) => T
items.summary: shrink_to_minimal: shrink a failing value to a minimal counterexample (spec §3). The result is the REIFIED minimal value (FLAG-testing-3 — Rust renders it into the description String).

items.signature: fn for_all_loop[T](gen: Binary{64} => GenStep[T], shrink: T => Vec[T], prop: T => Bool, state: Binary{64}, seed: Binary{64}, remaining: Binary{8}, trial: Binary{8}, generated_any: Bool) => Verdict[T]
items.summary: for_all_loop: the trial spine — generate, check, recurse. generated_any distinguishes "budget done, everything passed" (Pass) from "the generator never produced" (Skipped(NeedsRecord) — C1: a property that could not run is reported, never a silent pass).

items.signature: fn for_all[T](gen: Binary{64} => GenStep[T], shrink: T => Vec[T], seed: Binary{64}, budget: Budget, prop: T => Bool) => Verdict[T]
items.summary: for_all: run a property over `budget` generated inputs. Returns the first failure (shrunk to a minimal counterexample, with the reproducing seed + trial index), Skipped(NeedsRecord) if the generator cannot produce, or Pass. The gen/shrink fn pair substitutes the Gen<T> trait instance (FLAG-testing-1). Guarantee: Exact as a MECHANISM (the verdict is an exact function of the run); a passing for_all backs an EMPIRICAL claim about the property — never Proven; no op here performs that upgrade (C2/VR-5 — the crux this module exists to prevent).

items.signature: fn golden{N}(baseline: Option[GoldenBaseline[Binary{N}]], name: Binary{8}, produced: Binary{N}) => Verdict[GoldenFail[Binary{N}]]
fn diff_compare{N}(input_desc: Bytes, lhs_out: Binary{N}, rhs_out: Binary{N}) => Verdict[DiffFail[Binary{N}]]
fn differential{N}(input_desc: Bytes, lhs_available: Bool, lhs: Unit => Binary{N}, rhs_available: Bool, rhs: Unit => Binary{N}) => Verdict[DiffFail[Binary{N}]]
items.summary: differential: require lhs() == rhs() through two thunked backends. An unavailable backend is Skipped(BackendUnavailable) — NEVER a silent pass (C1/G2, the differential honesty crux); the thunks are only forced when both backends are available. Exact mechanism; the two-level §8-Q5 bar's tag/EXPLAIN level is the Rust crate's own FLAG-Q5, carried over unresolved.
summarize: aggregate verdicts keeping Skipped/Undetermined counts DISTINCT from Pass — "green" can never silently include "did not actually check" (C1/G2). Exact (a total function over verdicts).

is_green: True iff there are no failures. Skipped/undetermined counts are SURFACED in the Summary (the caller can inspect them), not hidden and not treated as failures — treating "could not run" as "failed" would itself violate C1 (the Rust doc's exact reasoning). Exact.
summary field accessors (the .myc idiom for Rust's named struct fields). Exact.

summary_total: the total verdict count. Substitution note: Rust saturates (saturating_add); add_u instead REFUSES past 255 on all paths — a louder, never-silent overflow discipline (G2), documented rather than hidden.

items.summary: scope_contains: True iff the mode is in scope. Exact (total, match-defined).

Pinned against the Rust resolver by the live oracle differential (FLAG-testing-7 — the KC-3 shared-resolver property does not carry over). Exact mechanism.

items.summary: config_with_granular: add/replace the granular per-test override — wins over all scope tiers. Exact.

config_provenance: the resolved mode + its source scope — the EXPLAIN of the scope decision (never ambient, G2). A granular override reports source None (above the lattice), exactly as in Rust. Exact.

items.summary: mode_list_eq: element-wise Vec[CertMode] equality. Exact.

Self-hosted UTF-8 utilities: byte length, slice/concat over the kernel Bytes, ASCII/continuation classification, bounds-checked byte access, full multi-byte UTF-8 codepoint decode (1/2/3/4-byte) WITH validity rejection (overlong / surrogate / >U+10FFFF — M-717 complete).

Scope (cumulative): byte_len, is_ascii_byte, byte_at, full decode_one (1/2/3/4-byte), and — added by DN-43/M-799 — `slice`/`concat` over the kernel `Bytes`. All operations that execute three-way are honest about their basis. ── Local Option (redeclared; no cross-leaf import) ────────────────────────────────────────────── Option<A>: the never-silent optional value. Redeclared here (not imported from std.option) to keep this nodule self-contained per worktree discipline. Semantics identical to std.option.

items.signature: type Utf8Error = Invalid(Binary{8}) | Overlong(Binary{8}) | Surrogate(Binary{8}) | TooLarge(Binary{8})
fn concat(b1: Bytes, b2: Bytes) => Bytes
fn slice(b: Bytes, start: Binary{32}, end: Binary{32}) => Bytes
items.summary: Guarantee: Exact over the in-range domain (`start <= end <= len`); the bounds contract is Declared/ never-silent (asserted + exhibited three-way by std_bytes_slice.rs, not machine-proven).

items.signature: fn slice_opt(b: Bytes, start: Binary{32}, end: Binary{32}) => Option[Bytes]
items.summary: Guarantee: Declared contract (the bounds predicate is Exact `lt`; the Option wrapping is the never-silent contract). Never-silent (G2): out-of-range/inverted ⇒ explicit `None`.

items.signature: fn byte_at(b: Bytes, i: Binary{8}) => Option[Binary{8}]
items.summary: Guarantee: Declared contract (the bounds predicate is Exact `lt`/`width_cast`; the Option wrapping is the never-silent contract). Never-silent (G2): an out-of-range index is an explicit `None`, never a kernel refusal leaking through and never a silent wrap. This closes FLAG-text-1 (wave-n1).

items.signature: fn reject_two(cp: Binary{32}, lead: Binary{8}) => Result[Pair[Binary{32}, Binary{8}], Utf8Error]
items.summary: reject_two/three/four take the ASSEMBLED codepoint `cp` (Binary{32}) and the sequence's lead byte, and return Ok(Pr(cp, width)) iff `cp` is the CANONICAL well-formed encoding for that byte width — else a never-silent Err carrying the lead byte (G2). They are the RFC-3629 well-formedness layer that the structural decode_two/three/four delegate their final Ok to. Comparisons are Exact `lt` at the common Binary{32} width; the bound constants are written as 32-bit BINARY literals (a `0x…` literal is a `Bytes` value in this lexer, not a Binary{32} number — so the thresholds use `0b…` explicitly): 0x80 = min 2-byte · 0x800 = min 3-byte · 0x10000 = min 4-byte (below ⇒ OVERLONG) · 0xD800..0xE000 = the surrogate gap (SURROGATE) · 0x10FFFF = the Unicode ceiling (above ⇒ TOOLARGE). Guarantee: Declared (the Result wrapping is the never-silent contract; the predicate is Exact `lt`). 2-byte: valid iff cp >= 0x80 (a 1-byte value here would be overlong). The 2-byte max (0x7FF) is below both the surrogate gap and the ceiling, so only the overlong check applies. (0x80 = 1<<7.)

items.signature: fn reject_three(cp: Binary{32}, lead: Binary{8}) => Result[Pair[Binary{32}, Binary{8}], Utf8Error]
items.summary: 3-byte: valid iff cp >= 0x800 (else overlong) AND cp not in 0xD800..0xDFFF (else a surrogate — not a Unicode scalar value). The 3-byte max (0xFFFF) is below the ceiling, so no too-large check. (0x800 = 1<<11; 0xD800 = surrogate-gap low; 0xE000 = surrogate-gap high+1.)

items.signature: fn reject_four(cp: Binary{32}, lead: Binary{8}) => Result[Pair[Binary{32}, Binary{8}], Utf8Error]
items.summary: 4-byte: valid iff cp >= 0x10000 (else overlong) AND cp <= 0x10FFFF (else above the Unicode ceiling). The 4-byte min (0x10000) is above the surrogate gap, so no surrogate check. (0x10000 = 1<<16; 0x10FFFF = the maximum Unicode scalar value.)

items.signature: fn decode_ascii(b: Bytes, i: Binary{8}) => Result[Binary{8}, Utf8Error]
items.summary: Guarantee: Declared (type-level contract; three-way differential agreement Empirical, std_text.rs).

items.signature: fn decode_two(b: Bytes, i: Binary{8}, lead: Binary{8}) => Result[Pair[Binary{32}, Binary{8}], Utf8Error]
items.summary: 2-byte: lead 0b110xxxxx, cont 0b10yyyyyy yields cp = (lead & 0x1F) << 6 | (cont & 0x3F).

items.signature: fn decode_three(b: Bytes, i: Binary{8}, lead: Binary{8}) => Result[Pair[Binary{32}, Binary{8}], Utf8Error]
items.summary: 3-byte: lead 0b1110xxxx, two conts yield cp = (lead & 0x0F) << 12 | (c1 & 0x3F) << 6 | (c2 & 0x3F).

items.signature: fn decode_four(b: Bytes, i: Binary{8}, lead: Binary{8}) => Result[Pair[Binary{32}, Binary{8}], Utf8Error]
items.summary: 4-byte: lead 0b11110xxx, three conts yield cp = (lead & 0x07) << 18 | (c1 & 0x3F) << 12 | (c2 & 0x3F) << 6 | (c3 & 0x3F).

items.signature: fn decode_one(b: Bytes, i: Binary{8}) => Result[Pair[Binary{32}, Binary{8}], Utf8Error]
items.summary: VALIDITY (M-717 — now CLOSED, the last M-717 remainder): the multi-byte arms reject OVERLONG encodings (a value encodable in fewer bytes), SURROGATE-range codepoints (U+D800–DFFF), and codepoints above U+10FFFF — each a never-silent `Err(Overlong/Surrogate/TooLarge(lead))` via the `reject_two/three/four` gates (the assembled codepoint compared against the per-length minimum and the surrogate/ceiling bounds with `lt` at Binary{32}). So `decode_one` yields only well-formed Unicode scalar values (RFC-3629). Structural malformations (bad lead, missing/short continuation, bad continuation) still surface as `Err(Invalid(byte))`. Boundary values (U+0080, U+0800, U+10000, U+10FFFF) are accepted, not over-rejected (std_text.rs validity tests).
